commit.message:
refactor: switch MU-loader from whitelist to blacklist architecture (v6.1.0)

Load all plugins by default, only restrict known-heavy ecosystems
(WooCommerce, LearnPress, bbPress, etc.) when their page conditions
aren't met. Eliminates silent plugin breakage when new plugins are
installed. All restriction rules are now DB-driven via scanner.

commit.message:
Hide empty standings table for years with no medals

When viewing a year with no medal activity (e.g., 2025), only show the
"No medals awarded yet" empty state instead of a full table of restaurants
with 0 scores. Medal year attribution is already correct — medals are
stored with a year field and past years are locked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.4.5: Workflow context, output parser fix, session cleanup (#63)

* refactor: consolidate duplicate code across providers and shared modules

- Extract isProcessAlive() and formatError() to shared/mcp-utils.ts
- Provider schemas now import sharedExecSchema/sharedListSchema/sharedForkSchema
- Bridge server imports waitInputSchema from shared (removes local zod dep)
- makeOnEvent() factory in providers/types.ts replaces duplicate in both adapters
- CallerContext: single definition in request-context.ts, re-exported from service.ts
- workflow/schemas.ts imports effortSchema from shared instead of inline enum
- progress-store: allocate read buffer locally, remove unused dir variable
- Remove unused NotifyFn and ProviderTool type exports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: rename working_directory to work_dir across MCP schemas

Shorter parameter name for all provider tools (codex, claude, workflow).
Internal cwd conversion unchanged. Also adds work_dir support to workflow tool.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: use selective Read for resolver output in --deep plan reviews

inline: false + Read by line range avoids loading architect/critic
raw output into orchestrator context when resolver already synthesized it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: consolidate 6 structural duplication patterns across providers

CLI detection: createCliDetector(config) factory in src/providers/cli-detection.ts
eliminates ~90 duplicated lines between codex and claude detection modules.
Thin wrappers preserve all type exports and public API.

Codex executor: positional parameters → CodexExecOptions bag, matching
Claude executor's existing pattern. executeFork prompt stays optional.

Adapter result mapping: mapProviderResultBase() extracts 4 common fields
(content, model, durationMs, aborted). Session policies stay per-adapter.

Lock file utility: tryExclusiveWrite() in shared/mcp-utils.ts replaces
two near-identical 15-line blocks in backend-lock.ts and backend-client.ts.

Pipe-parser quote scanner: scanQuoteAware(text, visitor) with raw-stream
contract replaces 4 copies of quote-tracking state machine. splitSteps
uses pendingDash pattern for -> detection.

In-memory caching: ProgressStore gets statusCache Map and O(1) liveJobCount().
SessionManager gets statSync-validated cache with copy-out semantics.
ProgressStore shared via constructor injection to ensure single authoritative
instance between server and ExecutionService.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove CLI detection wrappers, replace polling with event-driven wait

CLI detection: delete codex/cli-detection.ts and claude/cli-detection.ts
wrapper files. Move provider configs and parseClaudeAuthStatus into the
shared cli-detection.ts. Remove ClaudeCliInfo alias, unexport internal
types (AuthProbeResult, CliDetectorConfig, createCliDetector). Rename
resetCliCache to resetCodexCliCache for symmetry.

ProgressStore: add changeSeq-based waitForChange(sinceSeq) notification.
Writers (writeStatus, appendProgress) increment seq and wake waiters.
Callers snapshot seq before reading, pass it to waitForChange to avoid
missed-notification races.

ExecutionService: replace sleep(500ms) polling in waitStream and
awaitLaunch with ProgressStore.waitForChange(), falling back to
setTimeout for the remaining timeout window. Removes POLL_INTERVAL_MS
and sleep helper. service.test.ts drops from 2.3s to 1.1s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: improve plan skill — preplan integration, Task tracking, naming

- Rename {name} to {topic} for consistency with preplan convention
- Add topic derivation instruction when not invoked from preplan
- Add preplan path to plan stub structure
- Replace vague "preplan constraints" with explicit Success Criteria pass-through
- Add Task registration per review phase with round tracking in subject
- Clean up Tasks on completion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: enforce AskUserQuestion on every all-confirmed state in preplan

Move transition prompt into conversation loop so it fires every time
all items reach confirmed state, including after re-confirmation.
Add "Continue discussion" option. Deduplicate from Output_Format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: require reviewed plan path in architect/critic output format

Ensures resolver can reliably locate the plan file from upstream
output instead of relying on incidental path mentions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: implement design-improvements plan — 7 phases of execution layer cleanup

Consolidate execution authority into ProgressStore, dissolve JobManager into
abort-only AbortRegistry, bifurcate writeStatus (sync terminal / async non-terminal
with generation counter), and remove all test-only prod exports.

Phase 1: PersistedStatusRecord stores projectRoot for orphan recovery isolation
Phase 2: JobManager dissolved to AbortRegistry; ProgressStore sole state authority
Phase 3: writeStatus sync/async bifurcation with per-job write generation counter
Phase 4: /wait/stream SSE tracked by idle timer; projectRoot routed end-to-end
Phase 5: Zod schemas as single source for exec/resume/fork; internal fields
         (model, effort, bypass_permissions, system_prompt) hidden from MCP descriptors
Phase 6: Codex adapter preflight owns CLI detection; executor requires preChecked
Phase 7: All _test/_reset*ForTests exports removed; tests use vi.resetModules();
         Provider.capabilities removed; narrow WorkflowService interface

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: rename workflow prompt to init_prompt, add shared context field

Rename the workflow tool's `prompt` field to `init_prompt` to clarify it
only feeds step 0. Add optional `context` field that propagates to every
atom in every step, providing a shared context channel (plan path,
success criteria) that doesn't depend on upstream atom output.

Prompt composition order: context → stepPrompt → instruction.
First-step prompt literals receive context but not initialPrompt (by design).

Updated: schema, executor, service, server descriptor, plan skill
(semantic split: shared briefing → context, seed → init_prompt),
docs, KB, and all tests (5 files + new context propagation test).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add work_dir to workflow server descriptor

The workflow tool descriptor was missing work_dir in its advertised
properties, making the parameter undiscoverable to MCP clients despite
being accepted by the Zod schema.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: improve workflow arg semantics in plan skill

Split workflow call into three distinct roles:
- context: shared metadata (plan path, success criteria) for all atoms
- init_prompt: dynamic review briefing for step 0 reviewers only
- work_dir: actual process CWD (was embedded as text in context)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add descriptions to workflow tool descriptor and docs

Add description fields to all workflow tool properties in the backend
server descriptor. Add missing work_dir row to mcp-tools.md Input
Schema table. LLM clients can now discover what each parameter does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add prompt-mode iteration loop to coral:ralph

Extends coral:ralph with a Stop hook pattern that re-injects the same
prompt on each iteration, enabling ralph-loop style execution without a
plan file. The hook handles mode disambiguation by delegating to the LLM
(plan vs prompt), keeping the hook itself coarse-detection-only.

- hooks/ralph-loop.mjs (new): multi-event hook for UserPromptSubmit,
  PreToolUse(Skill), and Stop; creates session-scoped state file with
  defaults; re-injects prompt on Stop until maxIterations or promise match
- hooks/hooks.json: register ralph-loop.mjs on all three events (timeout 5s)
- hooks/kb-stale-cleanup.mjs: add ralph-state- prefix to stale cleanup
- skills/ralph/SKILL.md: add mode determination step (plan precedence +
  LLM judgment), --max-iterations/--completion-promise flags, step 7
  promise output rule
- docs/hooks.md: document new ralph-loop.mjs section and updated overview

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(kb): add Stop hook multi-block coexistence pattern

Confirmed during ralph-loop implementation: multiple Stop hooks each
returning decision:block work independently, each reason delivered as
a separate user prompt injection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: diet ralph SKILL.md — 177→127 lines (28% reduction)

Remove Verification Gate step (covered by Success_Criteria ALL_CAPS
constraint), compress Output_Format to generic placeholders, inline
scope gate path lists to reference project workflow, compress Codex
Post-Completion Review and Red_Attacker cross-model dispatch.

Restore cross-skill consistency: Constraints DO/DON'T table (4 rows,
unique guardrails only), Role agent name parentheticals, Output_Format
placeholder values matching plan/preplan structural pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: rename kb-stale-cleanup.mjs to stale-cleanup.mjs

Now cleans ralph-state-* files alongside kb-active-* and memo-reminded-*,
so the kb- prefix no longer reflects its scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: recover truncated response when Claude result contains only trailing Insight block

Claude CLI's stream-json result event captures only the last assistant turn.
When Explanatory output style produces a trailing ★ Insight block as a separate
turn, the actual review content is lost. Fix tracks per-message text and prepends
the previous assistant message when the result starts with an Insight marker.

Also handles leading \n\n in the result field (actual CLI wire format).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: walk back through consecutive Insight blocks instead of one-level recovery

When multiple trailing assistant turns are Insight blocks, the previous
fix only recovered one level. Now walks backwards until a non-Insight
message is found, concatenating all skipped messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: clean up Claude session files after successful workflow completion

Workflow-spawned Claude atoms create session .jsonl files that pollute
the user's /resume list. After pipeline completes successfully, fire-and-
forget cleanup removes these files. Session IDs are collected inside
waitForAtoms (not from launchedAtoms) so stale-recovered sessions with
updated IDs are also cleaned up. Failed pipelines preserve sessions for
debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* v0.4.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix: commit rendered pulse pages before opening PR in publish/approve_all

When running locally, rendered pages were left untracked and never committed
before open_daily_pr() was called, so the PR contained no content changes.

fix: install.sh always overwrite hooks/permissions/env from repo

Previously these fields were only added if missing, so updating
a hook type (command→prompt) wouldn't propagate to other machines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: GitHub Copilot API integration (#1)

* feat: switch LLM backend from Anthropic SDK to GitHub Copilot API

Platoon now authenticates and calls LLMs through the GitHub Copilot API
(same route as Squad) instead of the Anthropic SDK directly.

Changes:
- internal/agent/runner.go: replace AnthropicClient with CopilotClient
  using net/http; token resolution chain: config → GITHUB_TOKEN env →
  `gh auth token`; exchanges GitHub token for short-lived Copilot session
  token via api.github.com/copilot_internal/v2/token; caches token with
  2-min buffer before expiry
- internal/config/config.go: rename AnthropicAPIKey → GitHubToken
  (github_token); default model claude-opus-4-6 → claude-sonnet-4.5
- cmd/init.go: auto-detect `gh auth token` and confirm existing Copilot
  credentials; fall back to manual GitHub PAT entry
- cmd/run.go: use NewCopilotClient instead of NewAnthropicClient
- internal/config/config_test.go: update field names and model strings
- go.mod: remove anthropic-sdk-go dependency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address all PR review comments + rename .sponge/ → .platoon/

Protocol directory:
- Rename .sponge/ → .platoon/ throughout (repo.go, init.go, repos.go,
  root.go, models.go); rename InitSpongeDir → InitPlatoonDir

PR comment fixes:
- cmd/agents.go: agents edit now derives default charter path
  (~/.platoon/agents/{name}.md) when CharterFile is empty, instead of
  treating it as "agent not found"
- cmd/status.go: countInboxOrders now uses IsOrderEntry() to skip the
  seed comment block instead of the broken len(entry) > 0 check
- internal/config/config.go: use errors.As(err, &viper.ConfigFileNotFoundError)
  alongside os.IsNotExist so first-run works without a config file
- internal/agent/runner.go: release mutex before the HTTP POST so all
  agents run in true parallel (lock only for token refresh)
- internal/report/parser.go: ParseOutbox skips comment-only seed blocks
  (blocks with no markdown heading); IsProcessedBy and IsRelayedTo add
  a space delimiter after the name/target to prevent prefix false positives;
  add IsOrderEntry() helper for inbox counting
- internal/agent/agent.go: fix fallback model from claude-opus-4-6 to
  claude-sonnet-4.5 to match config default
- internal/report/writer.go: validate agentName in MarkProcessed to
  reject names containing newlines or "-->" (injection prevention)
- internal/repo/repo.go: createIfNotExists now checks os.IsNotExist
  explicitly and surfaces other stat errors instead of swallowing them
- internal/agent/copilot_test.go: add httptest-based unit tests for
  CopilotClient covering token exchange, caching, refresh, 200/non-200
  completions, empty choices, and error payloads
- internal/agent/runner_test.go: update model name to claude-sonnet-4.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Send plain YYYY-MM-DD ship date without time component

Time suffixes (T12:00:00Z, T12:00:00) were still causing date shifts.
Now sends bare date string '2026-03-09' to ShipStation V2 API which
is the bridge to Amazon Buy Shipping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Enable autoCompact for headless sessions (#1888)

Headless sessions (`-p --input-format stream-json`) don't auto-compact
by default, causing "Prompt is too long" synthetic errors when
conversations approach the 200K context window. This was especially
problematic for forked sessions that inherit a large parent context.

Midtown passes `--setting-sources project,local` (excluding user-level
settings) and its own `--settings` file, so the user's autoCompact
preference was never observed. Adding `autoCompact: true` to the shared
common settings ensures all sessions compact before hitting the limit.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Re-sign items with local signing identity before pushing to peers

Items in the DB may carry another node's pubkey/signature. Without
re-signing, pushing shared items just echoes the original signer back,
preventing multi-signer trust from working bidirectionally.

commit.message:
Unify Training Suite drill cards with consistent gold/dark casino theme

feat: Palantir mode — sacrifice-aware smart reciprocation engine

Adds a third reciprocation mode that calculates optimal send-back
amounts using donor sacrifice ratio, loyalty (repeat sends), teammate
bonus, and power-phase self-preservation. One button, zero config.

Formula: sacrifice * loyalty * team * phase, with 10K floor and 40%
cap (cap wins over floor). UI shows live decision feed with sacrifice
bars, loyalty dots, phase badges, and full formula breakdown.

26 new unit tests for the pure formula function.

feat(preflight): --proof flag runs research before re-scoring

kiln preflight --proof:

  2. If ambiguity >= 1 or score 3-5: extract unknowns + run proof in parallel
  3. Write findings to BeadStore (preflight-<run-id>-<group> node IDs)

  5. Print fast → refined score delta

commit.message:
feat: 3a IonQ JSON import/export — Circuit.fromIonQ / circuit.toIonQ

Serializes the IonQ native gate set to ionq.circuit.v0 JSON.  Angle
convention: rotation gates use π-radians (θ/π), native-gate phases use
turns (φ/2π), matching the IonQ Cloud API spec.

Gate metadata is carried on ops at construction time (GateMeta tag on
SingleOp/TwoOp) so toIonQ() never needs to invert matrices.  Unsupported
gates (controlled variants, U-gates, XY/iSWAP, mid-circuit ops) throw
TypeError with a clear message.

Round-trip tested across all gate families; parametric angles recover
to machine precision.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

the user has just logged out.

commit.message:
fix: hasCommitForIssue regex so issues actually get closed

The \b word boundary after #N doesn't work in git extended-regexp
because # is a non-word character. Changed to #N[^0-9] (non-digit
after) with a fallback for #N at end of line. This was the root cause
of issues never being auto-closed by the orchestrator.

commit.message:
fix: unify action buttons to btn-ghost style

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace regex rewrites with Claude-powered context-aware rewrites

The old regex approach blindly swapped "may"→"will", "believe"→"see",
etc. which broke safe harbor language, created grammatical errors, and
turned cautionary disclosures into definitive claims.

Now Claude reviews all flagged sentences in a batch with explicit rules:
- Never touch safe harbor / legal boilerplate
- Never change intentional risk disclosures
- Only rewrite hedging that genuinely weakens the message
- Preserve grammar and original meaning

Q&A and rewrite Claude calls run in parallel via asyncio.gather so
total latency doesn't increase.

commit.message:
feat: redesign ProfilePage with tabbed edit mode and clean view

- Hero card: compact avatar + name + role + location at a glance
- Edit mode: 4 tabs (Основное / Профессия / Поиск / Соцсети)
  each section clearly separated, no more one giant form
- View mode: chip-based info display, clickable social links
- Avatar upload always accessible without entering edit mode
- Save button prominent at bottom of edit mode
- Reduced from 1048 to ~450 lines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace pbcopy/pbpaste with native macOS Pasteboard API for UTF-8 clipboard

French accented characters (è, à, ç) were corrupted when pasted into Chrome
and Firestorm. The old pbcopy shell-out may misinterpret UTF-8 bytes based
on process locale. The new implementation uses PasteboardCreate/PutItemFlavor
with explicit "public.utf8-plain-text" flavor, guaranteeing correct encoding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ZED IMU QoS: use BEST_EFFORT to match ZED wrapper publisher

ZED ROS2 wrapper publishes sensor data with BEST_EFFORT QoS.
A RELIABLE subscriber silently receives nothing from a BEST_EFFORT
publisher in DDS, causing the dashboard to never see IMU data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update dashboard progress section - fix capitalization, rename seniority levels, remove solve case button

- Change "Prawo Cywilne" to "Prawo cywilne" etc.
- Replace Junior/Mid/Senior with Początkujący/Podstawowy/Średniozaawansowany/Zaawansowany/Ekspert
- Remove "Rozwiąż nowy kazus" button

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove SUNO-PROMPTS.md from repo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
